Lore Item No Trade
Slot: LEGS
AC: 44
HP: +55
Effect: Enduring Breath (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 2.5 Size: TINY
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD BRD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Slot: LEGS
AC: 44
HP: +55
Effect: Enduring Breath (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)

Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-12 08:20:22
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 58

Noobies
QuoteReply
# Feb 25 2002 at 10:33 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750368914
41 posts
This item looks to drop in north ToV... which means you'll never see it unless you get off these boards and play a LOT more. Also Monks can't use these.... which makes it somewhat stupid to give to a monk. I play a monk and a Druid and I hate noobies that say stuff like "Doodz this is a UBER MONK item.... Look how much it weights" If you were able to come get this item you would know that if you got agro where it droped, One, you really suck at Agro Management. and two, That if you go get agro you will be seeing "Loading Please Wait"

MAN I AM TIRED OF MONKS
ok I am a pally, and I am REALY glad that my guild only has 3 monks. (o BTW I am flaming now). Because if I had anymore i would swear I will never get any good gear. OK i know i know it's carring on this stupid post string.. as a matter of fact i am gonna have to post this somewhere esle also.

MONKS FIGHT LIKE A MONK!!! Aren't you all supposed to be running about in orange robes or something?

# Oct 21 2003 at 5:46 PM Rating: Decent

126 posts
Hello. My name is Ulic, and I am a loot *****. I tried converting to monkhood. I tried a 12-step program. I tried crying myself to sleep. Nothing works...I'm still a loot *****.

I say band together with your fellow loot whores of all classes and create a guild! Let the loot flow and the whores be sedated; only then will there be peace in BoT!

Hey, nice greaves!

# May 18 2001 at 3:44 PM Rating: Default

Hey, nice greaves!
I bet that any lucky guy who got these, wont prolly get better ones untill he really starts to bother him/her self with the uber armor quests..
▲ Page top
funny weights
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 4:38 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Sorry, this post is a sort of flame, though I'm really curious why people refer to items by "stone" weight, a real "stone" is the equivalent of 14 pounds what I want to know is why people refer to it as this, pounds would make much more sense. think of it this way monks can carry like 14 "units" of weight without being hindered (AC hits) that is like saying a monk could fight like a martial artist with 160 or so pounds on his back. That does'nt even taking into account when my shadow knight carries 130 "units" if it were true stones he would be carrying 1820 pounds!! oh well, it does'nt make a difference either way, just seems like saying stones to be "more in character" just make weights and measures seem flawed in norrath :-P
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 20 2001 at 3:58 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Personally I think that it is funny that you are questioning the weight units used... in a game where one can fight dragons and giants, and can cast spells that can make themselves stronger, or even change the weather. So you're telling me in a world where all of this is plausible, that carrying 1,820 pounds isn't possible? Let me remind you that also in this world exists Ogres, Trolls, and Elfs... :)
RE: funny weights
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 9:02 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Ehhem one wt unit whatever you wish to call it is 10 pounds in this game, in cabalis you can catch a ten pound trout, this is the name, and the weight listed is 1.0, there is also somewhere a ten pound stone, also wt 1.0, I don't know why the term stone came into use but I would assume it has to do with the prevelence of the weight measurment stones in most fantasy type games and books... just my 2 cents
▲ Page top
RE: funny weights
QuoteReply
# Mar 23 2003 at 7:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783655105
8 posts
well... if wt 1.0 is equal to 10 pounds, then i think we all have supernatural strength. I see quite a few items weighing 20 or more.
Lets take up the monk as an example. What is the weight limit for a monk, before he or she loses ac... 15'ish?`... well.. that would be the equivalent to 150 pounds (70 kilos) of clothing and other goodies. Imagine doing those flying kicks on a 3 min ling fight (3 rl mins = 1 hour in game)... I know i'd be mighty tired.
So my conclusion is that a wt of 1.0 is equal to 1 pound.
▲ Page top
RE: funny weights
QuoteReply

then how come halas 10lb meat pies have a weight of 7.3? ;)
